task_completion.description: Open Panoply and load the air.mon.ltm.nc dataset
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply application window is open, and 'air.mon.ltm.nc' is visible in the datasets or sources list.

task_completion.description: Create a diagnostic map for April using a North Polar projection
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window showing a map centered on the North Pole (Greenland visible) with the time/array index set to April (e.g., Month 4).

task_completion.description: Create a diagnostic map for July using a North Polar projection
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window showing a map centered on the North Pole with the time/array index set to July (e.g., Month 7).

task_completion.description: Save both diagnostic maps to the GrISMelt directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window showing 'greenland_temp_april.png' and 'greenland_temp_july.png' successfully saved in the '~/Documents/GrISMelt/' directory.

task_completion.description: Write the ablation onset report with correct physical conclusions
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'ablation_onset_report.txt' in '~/Documents/GrISMelt/'. The text must correctly conclude that melting occurs in July (at least on the margins) and that April is frozen, demonstrating the agent realized the dataset values are in Celsius.

integrity.description: Agent used the Panoply GUI to generate the maps
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent interacting with the Panoply graphical interface to configure projections and export plots, rather than using Python scripts or terminal commands to bypass the tool.

integrity.description: The exported maps contain genuine Panoply plot data
integrity.visual_evidence: The PNG files or plot previews must visually depict a North Polar projection with temperature color scales, not blank images, generic maps, or copied files.

integrity.description: Agent interpreted the data correctly instead of blindly following the deceptive briefing
integrity.visual_evidence: The report should not claim the entire planet is frozen (which happens if raw Celsius values are naively compared to 273.15K). The agent must show evidence of recognizing the data is in Celsius (e.g., noting temps around 0-11 degrees).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset 'air.mon.ltm.nc' contains temperature values in Celsius, not Kelvin. The true mean July air temperature over Greenland has margins reaching up to 11.8°C (melting) and an interior dropping to -6.9°C (frozen). In April, the region is overwhelmingly frozen. The agent must correctly identify that melting occurs in July but not April. If the agent naively compares the raw dataset values against 273.15, they will incorrectly conclude the entire planet is frozen. The report must reflect the correct physical state (melting in July, frozen in April) and be saved as 'ablation_onset_report.txt'. The maps must be saved as 'greenland_temp_april.png' and 'greenland_temp_july.png' in '~/Documents/GrISMelt/'.
